title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · API and Operations Recall
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs namespaces, metadata, labels, selectors, ownership, and deletion from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the resource identity and lifecycle policy under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for resource identity and lifecycle policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish namespaced and cluster-scoped resources, default namespaces, cross-namespace references, quotas, and isolation limits.
Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.
Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery
steps.instruction:
review case — A review namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely.

Inspect the K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret deletiontimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, keep K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: For K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A review namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Deleting finalizers immediately is the normal first repair for any terminating resource.

lines.label: assumption

options.text: The K8s Finalizers Deletion Recovery record says resource identity and lifecycle policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
